report_name: Supply Chain Breach at Klue Exposes Salesforce Data of Huntress, HackerOne, and Others

threat_actors.name: Icarus
threat_actors.description: A new extortion group that conducted a multi-stage supply chain attack against Klue by exploiting a legacy service account credential to harvest Salesforce OAuth tokens and exfiltrate CRM data from nearly 200 downstream customers. The group was reportedly hacked by a second, unidentified party during its operation.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through the theft and threatened release of sensitive CRM data.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The Icarus group exploited a legacy service account credential for an abandoned but active integration to gain initial access to Klue's backend systems.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: Attackers pushed a malicious code update to the Klue SaaS platform, modifying the server-side application logic to intercept and collect customer OAuth tokens.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Application Access Token
attack_patterns.description: The core of the attack involved harvesting Salesforce OAuth tokens from Klue's customers by deploying malicious code on Klue's backend.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Using stolen OAuth tokens, the attackers made authorized API calls to customers' Salesforce environments to collect sensitive CRM data, including business contacts and sales data.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Exfiltration
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used the stolen OAuth tokens and authorized API calls to exfiltrate valuable business contact and sales data from victims' Salesforce instances, blending in with legitimate application traffic.

identities.name: Klue
identities.description: A competitive intelligence SaaS provider that was the primary target of a supply chain attack, leading to data exposure for its customers.

identities.name: Huntress
identities.description: A cybersecurity firm and Klue customer whose Salesforce data was exposed in the breach.

identities.name: HackerOne
identities.description: A cybersecurity firm and Klue customer whose Salesforce data was exposed in the breach.

identities.name: Recorded Future
identities.description: A cybersecurity firm and Klue customer whose Salesforce data was exposed in the breach.

identities.name: Tanium
identities.description: A cybersecurity firm and Klue customer whose Salesforce data was exposed in the breach.

identities.name: Jamf
identities.description: A software company and Klue customer whose Salesforce data was exposed in the breach.

identities.name: Snyk
identities.description: A cybersecurity firm and Klue customer whose Salesforce data was exposed in the breach.

identities.name: Salesforce
identities.description: The CRM platform from which customer data was exfiltrated. The attackers abused its API via stolen OAuth tokens from Klue's integration.

indicators.name: Unusual Volume of OAuth Refresh Token Grants
indicators.pattern:
title: Unusual Volume of OAuth Refresh Token Grants
id: 9b1b3e4a-1f1a-4b9a-8c1a-9d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: >
  Monitors for a high volume of OAuth token refresh requests. A spike in this activity could indicate an attacker attempting to maintain persistent access using stolen tokens, as seen in the Klue supply chain attack.
references:
  - https://www.rescana.com/post/klue-supply-chain-breach-exposes-oauth-tokens-and-salesforce-data-in-multi-stage-cybersecurity-incident-june-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-29
tags:
  - attack.t1528
  - attack.credential_access
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - 'grant_type=refresh_token'
  # Note: This is a basic rule. A robust implementation requires time-series analysis to detect unusual volume against a baseline.
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects OAuth token refresh requests. A high volume of these requests could indicate an attacker attempting to maintain persistent access using stolen tokens, as seen in the Klue supply chain attack.

relationships.source_name: Unusual Volume of OAuth Refresh Token Grants
